subject: fuse/io-uring: Proposal to support pBuf in additon to kBuf

reviews.author: Bernd Schubert
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Bernd Schubert argued that having many small requests is efficient, while large requests are valuable and should not be stalled by waiting for smaller buffers to become available. He also pointed out that the proposed design would add kernel-side complexity due to per-pool wait queue management.

Reviewer Bernd Schubert expressed concerns that the multi-buf-size-per-queue design suboptimally distributes buffers, causing fragmentation and inefficiency in buffer utilization. He argued that separate payload-size queues would allow for better distribution of buffers and more efficient use of available capacity.
reviews.raw_body:
My counter argument is that it is simple to have many small requests and
large requests are precious :)

---

[ Right now no time answer to all the other parts ].

I definitely agree that we disagree here :)

Yeah, I think it would be good to discuss this in person and also in the
afternoon session on Wednesday.


Thanks,
Bernd

2026-04-30.patch_summary: This patch proposes to support pBuf in addition to kBuf in the fuse/io-uring interface, allowing for more efficient handling of small requests and reducing kernel-side complexity. The current implementation using multiple queues for different buffer sizes is seen as suboptimal due to fragmentation and inefficient distribution of buffers. The proposed solution involves separate payload-size queues, which simplifies kernel-side logic and improves performance. This change aims to address issues with head-of-line blocking and non-blocking IO, making it more feasible for fuse servers to use io-uring without requiring significant modifications.
